name: Cognitive Abilities
description: Abilities that influence the acquisition and application of knowledge in problem solving

name: Verbal Abilities
description: Abilities that influence the acquisition and application of verbal information in problem solving

name: Oral Comprehension
description: The ability to listen to and understand information and ideas presented through spoken words and sentences.

scale_anchors.description: Understand a television commercial
Understand a coach's oral instructions for a sport
Understand a lecture on advanced physics

name: Written Comprehension
description: The ability to read and understand information and ideas presented in writing.

scale_anchors.description: Understand signs on the highway
Understand an apartment lease
Understand an instruction book on repairing Artificial Intelligence systems

name: Oral Expression
description: The ability to communicate information and ideas in speaking so others will understand.

scale_anchors.description: Place an order at a restaurant drive-thru
Give instructions to a lost motorist
Explain advanced principles of genetics to college freshmen

name: Written Expression
description: The ability to communicate information and ideas in writing so others will understand.

scale_anchors.description: Write a note to remind someone to take food out of the freezer
Write a job recommendation for a subordinate
Write an advanced economics textbook

name: Idea Generation and Reasoning Abilities
description: Abilities that influence the application and manipulation of information in problem solving

name: Fluency of Ideas
description: The ability to come up with a number of ideas about a topic (the number of ideas is important, not their quality, correctness, or creativity).

scale_anchors.description: Name four different uses for a screwdriver
Think of as many ideas as possible for the name of a new company
Identify as many strategies as possible for a company acquisition and merger

name: Originality
description: The ability to come up with unusual or clever ideas about a given topic or situation, or to develop creative ways to solve a problem.

scale_anchors.description: Use a credit card to open a locked door
Redesign job tasks to be interesting for employees
Invent a new type of man-made fiber

name: Problem Sensitivity
description: The ability to tell when something is wrong or is likely to go wrong. It does not involve solving the problem, only recognizing that there is a problem.

scale_anchors.description: Recognize that an unplugged lamp won't work
Notice from a subtle difference in a client's mood that something is bothering them
Recognize an illness at an early stage of a disease when there are only a few symptoms

name: Deductive Reasoning
description: The ability to apply general rules to specific problems to produce answers that make sense.

scale_anchors.description: Know that a stalled car can coast downhill
Decide what factors to consider in selecting stocks
Design an aircraft wing using principles of aerodynamics

name: Inductive Reasoning
description: The ability to combine pieces of information to form general rules or conclusions (includes finding a relationship among seemingly unrelated events).

scale_anchors.description: Decide what to wear based on the weather report
Determine the prime suspect based on crime scene evidence
Diagnose a disease using results of many different lab tests

name: Information Ordering
description: The ability to arrange things or actions in a certain order or pattern according to a specific rule or set of rules (e.g., patterns of numbers, letters, words, pictures, mathematical operations).

scale_anchors.description: Put things in numerical order
Follow the correct steps to make change
Assemble a wind turbine

name: Category Flexibility
description: The ability to generate or use different sets of rules for combining or grouping things in different ways.

scale_anchors.description: Sort paintbrushes based on length
Classify flowers according to size, color, and smell
Classify man-made fibers in terms of their characteristics, like strength or cost

name: Quantitative Abilities
description: Abilities that influence the solution of problems involving mathematical relationships

name: Mathematical Reasoning
description: The ability to choose the right mathematical methods or formulas to solve a problem.

scale_anchors.description: Determine how much 20 oranges will cost when they are priced at 5 for 4 dollars
Decide how to calculate profits to determine the amounts of yearly bonuses
Determine the mathematics required to simulate a space craft landing on the moon

name: Number Facility
description: The ability to add, subtract, multiply, or divide quickly and correctly.

scale_anchors.description: Add 2 and 7
Calculate a discounted price
Compute the interest payment that should be generated from an investment

name: Memory
description: Abilities related to the recall of available information

name: Memorization
description: The ability to remember information such as words, numbers, pictures, and procedures.

scale_anchors.description: Remember the number on your bus to be sure you get back on the right one
Recite the first names of the five people you just met
Recite a 300-word speech after studying it for 15 minutes

name: Perceptual Abilities
description: Abilities related to the acquisition and organization of visual information

name: Speed of Closure
description: The ability to quickly make sense of, combine, and organize information into meaningful patterns.

scale_anchors.description: Recognize a song after hearing only the first few notes
Make sense of messy handwriting
Interpret patterns on weather radar to decide if the weather is changing

name: Flexibility of Closure
description: The ability to identify or detect a known pattern (a figure, object, word, or sound) that is hidden in other distracting material.

scale_anchors.description: Detect that your phone is ringing while at a concert
Look for a golf ball in tall grass
Identify camouflaged animals from a moving vehicle while on a safari

name: Perceptual Speed
description: The ability to quickly and accurately compare similarities and differences among sets of letters, numbers, objects, pictures, or patterns. The things to be compared may be presented at the same time or one after the other. This ability also includes comparing a presented object with a remembered object.

scale_anchors.description: Sort mail according to ZIP codes with no time pressure
Read five pressure indicators in 10 seconds to make sure each is within safe limits
Inspect electrical parts for defects as they pass on a fast-moving assembly line

name: Spatial Abilities
description: Abilities related to the manipulation and organization of spatial information

name: Spatial Orientation
description: The ability to know your location in relation to the environment or to know where other objects are in relation to you.

scale_anchors.description: Use the floor plan to locate a store in a mall
Find your way through a dark room without hitting anything
Navigate an ocean voyage using a compass and landmarks

name: Visualization
description: The ability to imagine how something will look after it is moved around or when its parts are moved or rearranged.

scale_anchors.description: Imagine how to place paper in a printer so that the letterhead comes out on top
Follow a diagram to assemble a metal storage cabinet
Anticipate future moves in a chess game

name: Attentiveness
description: Abilities related to application of attention

name: Selective Attention
description: The ability to concentrate on a task over a period of time without being distracted.

scale_anchors.description: Answer a business call with coworkers talking nearby
Watch security monitors for intruders throughout the night shift
Study a technical manual while listening to loud construction sounds

name: Time Sharing
description: The ability to shift back and forth between two or more activities or sources of information (such as speech, sounds, touch, or other sources).

scale_anchors.description: Listen to music while entering numbers into a spreadsheet
Watch street signs while driving at 30 miles an hour
Monitor radar and radio transmissions to keep track of heavy aircraft traffic

name: Psychomotor Abilities
description: Abilities that influence the capacity to manipulate and control objects

name: Fine Manipulative Abilities
description: Abilities related to the manipulation of objects

name: Arm-Hand Steadiness
description: The ability to keep your hand and arm steady while moving your arm or while holding your arm and hand in one position.

scale_anchors.description: Light a candle
Thread a needle
Cut facets in a diamond

name: Manual Dexterity
description: The ability to quickly move your hand, your hand together with your arm, or your two hands to grasp, manipulate, or assemble objects.

scale_anchors.description: Screw a light bulb into a light socket
Pack oranges in crates as quickly as possible
Perform open heart surgery with surgical instruments

name: Finger Dexterity
description: The ability to make precisely coordinated movements of the fingers of one or both hands to grasp, manipulate, or assemble very small objects.

scale_anchors.description: Put coins in a parking meter
Attach small dials to audio equipment on an assembly line
Put together the inner workings of a small wrist watch

name: Control Movement Abilities
description: Abilities related to the control and manipulation of objects in time and space

name: Control Precision
description: The ability to quickly and repeatedly adjust the controls of a machine or a vehicle to exact positions.

scale_anchors.description: Adjust a room light with a dimmer switch
Adjust farm tractor controls
Drill a tooth

name: Multilimb Coordination
description: The ability to coordinate two or more limbs (for example, two arms, two legs, or one leg and one arm) while sitting, standing, or lying down. It does not involve performing the activities while the whole body is in motion.

scale_anchors.description: Row a boat
Operate a forklift truck in a warehouse
Play the drum set in a jazz band

name: Response Orientation
description: The ability to choose quickly between two or more movements in response to two or more different signals (lights, sounds, pictures). It includes the speed with which the correct response is started with the hand, foot, or other body part.

scale_anchors.description: When a doorbell and phone ring at the same time, quickly select which to answer first
Use either the brake or gas pedal when your vehicle is skidding
In an out-of-control commercial aircraft, react quickly to restore control

name: Rate Control
description: The ability to time your movements or the movement of a piece of equipment in anticipation of changes in the speed and/or direction of a moving object or scene.

scale_anchors.description: Ride a bicycle alongside a jogger
Keep up with a car that changes speed
Shoot a duck in flight

name: Reaction Time and Speed Abilities
description: Abilities related to speed of manipulation of objects

name: Reaction Time
description: The ability to quickly respond (with the hand, finger, or foot) to a signal (sound, light, picture) when it appears.

scale_anchors.description: Start to slow down the car when a traffic light turns yellow
Press a button to stop operation when a warning light goes off
Hit the brake when a pedestrian steps in front of your car

name: Wrist-Finger Speed
description: The ability to make fast, simple, repeated movements of the fingers, hands, and wrists.

scale_anchors.description: Use a screwdriver to twist in a screw
Carve roast beef in a cafeteria
Type 90 words per minute

name: Speed of Limb Movement
description: The ability to quickly move the arms and legs.

scale_anchors.description: Saw through a thin piece of wood
Swat a fly with a fly swatter
Throw punches in a boxing match

name: Physical Abilities
description: Abilities that influence strength, endurance, flexibility, balance and coordination

name: Physical Strength Abilities
description: Abilities related to the capacity to exert force

name: Static Strength
description: The ability to exert maximum muscle force to lift, push, pull, or carry objects.

scale_anchors.description: Push an empty shopping cart
Pull a 40-pound sack of fertilizer across the lawn
Lift 75-pound bags of cement onto a truck

name: Explosive Strength
description: The ability to use short bursts of muscle force to propel oneself (as in jumping or sprinting), or to throw an object.

scale_anchors.description: Hit a nail with a hammer
Jump onto a 3-foot high platform
Throw a shot-put in a track meet

name: Dynamic Strength
description: The ability to exert muscle force repeatedly or continuously over time. This involves muscular endurance and resistance to muscle fatigue.

scale_anchors.description: Use pruning shears to trim a bush
Climb a 48-foot long ladder
Perform a gymnastics routine using the rings

name: Trunk Strength
description: The ability to use your abdominal and lower back muscles to support part of the body repeatedly or continuously over time without "giving out" or fatiguing.

scale_anchors.description: Sit up in an office chair
Shovel snow for half an hour
Do 100 sit-ups

name: Endurance
description: The ability to exert oneself physically over long periods without getting out of breath

name: Stamina
description: The ability to exert yourself physically over long periods of time without getting winded or out of breath.

scale_anchors.description: Walk 1/4 mile
Climb 6 flights of stairs
Run 10 miles

name: Flexibility, Balance, and Coordination
description: Abilities related to the control of gross body movements

name: Extent Flexibility
description: The ability to bend, stretch, twist, or reach with your body, arms, and/or legs.

scale_anchors.description: Reach to adjust the radio in a car
Reach for a box on a high warehouse shelf
Work under the dashboard of a car

name: Dynamic Flexibility
description: The ability to quickly and repeatedly bend, stretch, twist, or reach out with your body, arms, and/or legs.

scale_anchors.description: Hand pick a bushel of apples from a tree
Perform a dance routine as part of a cheerleading squad
Maneuver a kayak through swift rapids

name: Gross Body Coordination
description: The ability to coordinate the movement of your arms, legs, and torso together when the whole body is in motion.

scale_anchors.description: Get in and out of a truck
Swim the length of a pool
Perform a ballet dance

name: Gross Body Equilibrium
description: The ability to keep or regain your body balance or stay upright when in an unstable position.

scale_anchors.description: Stand on a ladder
Walk on ice across a pond
Walk on narrow beams in high-rise construction

name: Sensory Abilities
description: Abilities that influence visual, auditory and speech perception

name: Visual Abilities
description: Abilities related to visual sensory input

name: Near Vision
description: The ability to see details at close range (within a few feet of the observer).

scale_anchors.description: Read indicators on the dashboard of a car
Read the fine print of a legal document
Detect minor defects in a diamond

name: Far Vision
description: The ability to see details at a distance.

scale_anchors.description: Read a roadside billboard
Read the slides on a presentation from across a room
Detect differences in ships on the horizon

name: Visual Color Discrimination
description: The ability to match or detect differences between colors, including shades of color and brightness.

scale_anchors.description: Separate laundry into colors and whites
Trace electrical circuits marked by various colored wires
Paint a color portrait of a person

name: Night Vision
description: The ability to see under low-light conditions.

scale_anchors.description: Read street signs at dusk (just after sunset)
Read a book in a dimly lit setting
Find your way through the woods on a moonless night

name: Peripheral Vision
description: The ability to see objects or movement of objects to one's side when the eyes are looking ahead.

scale_anchors.description: Keep in step while marching in a band
Be aware of the location of your teammates while dribbling a basketball
Distinguish a teammate from a competitor passing by in a car race

name: Depth Perception
description: The ability to judge which of several objects is closer or farther away from you, or to judge the distance between you and an object.

scale_anchors.description: Merge a car into traffic on a city street
Operate a crane to move materials from a truck bed to the ground
Throw a long pass to a closely guarded teammate

name: Glare Sensitivity
description: The ability to see objects in the presence of a glare or bright lighting.

scale_anchors.description: Drive on a familiar road on a cloudy day
See boats on the horizon when sailing
Snow ski in bright sunlight

name: Auditory and Speech Abilities
description: Abilities related to auditory and oral input

name: Hearing Sensitivity
description: The ability to detect or tell the differences between sounds that vary in pitch and loudness.

scale_anchors.description: Notice a doorbell ringing
Diagnose what's wrong with a car engine from its sound
Tune an orchestra

name: Auditory Attention
description: The ability to focus on a single source of sound in the presence of other distracting sounds.

scale_anchors.description: Listen to a lecture while people nearby are talking
Listen for your flight announcement at a busy airport
Listen to instructions from a coworker in a noisy saw mill

name: Sound Localization
description: The ability to tell the direction from which a sound originated.

scale_anchors.description: Listen to a sound system to determine which speaker is working
Find a ringing phone in an unfamiliar apartment
Determining the direction of an emergency vehicle from the sound of the siren

name: Speech Recognition
description: The ability to identify and understand the speech of another person.

scale_anchors.description: Recognize the voice of a coworker
Identify a former customer's voice over the phone
Understand a speech presented by someone with an unfamiliar accent

name: Speech Clarity
description: The ability to speak clearly so others can understand you.

scale_anchors.description: Call numbers in a bingo game
Make stadium-wide announcements during a sporting event
Give a lecture to a large audience
